remission.spontaneous_remission_rate: Childhood ITP: spontaneous remission in 70-80% within 6 months; Adult ITP: spontaneous remission in 20-30% at 1 year without treatment; chronic ITP (>12 months) rarely resolves spontaneously
remission.best_intervention_remission_rate: Rituximab: 40-60% initial response, 20-25% durable at 5 years; thrombopoietin receptor agonists (TPO-RA: eltrombopag, romiplostim): 70-90% response rate; splenectomy: 60-70% long-term complete response; fostamatinib: 18% CR in refractory; avatrombopag and lusutrombopag in perioperative settings
remission.gap_size: Moderate — TPO-RAs are highly effective but chronic use required in most; splenectomy (durable cure in 65%) is underused in appropriate patients
remission.barrier_detail: Splenectomy offers durable cure in 65% of ITP patients but is increasingly deferred in favour of chronic TPO-RA therapy which requires lifelong administration; the optimal sequence (corticosteroids → rituximab → TPO-RA → splenectomy) is debated; overtreated mild cases (platelet >30K without bleeding) remain a clinical challenge
remission.last_soc_change: 2023 — rilzabrutinib (BTK inhibitor) Phase 3 LUNA data; daprodustat + avatrombopag combinations; 2021 fostamatinib (SYK inhibitor) added; 2008 romiplostim/eltrombopag first-line TPO-RAs; splenectomy standard since early 20th century
remission.outdated_treatments: Danazol for chronic ITP (androgenic side effects; modest efficacy; dexamethasone or rituximab preferred); long-term OCS without steroid-sparing agent (prolonged corticosteroid use in ITP causes substantial morbidity); vinca alkaloids (vincristine/vinblastine) as emergency treatment (IV TPO-RA and IVIG preferred in acute setting)
remission.adjunctive_emerging: H. pylori eradication in H. pylori-positive ITP (remission in 30-50% — underperformed globally); low-dose naltrexone (LDN) in immune dysregulation; TPO-RA tapering protocols for drug-free remission induction
remission.frontier_signal: Rilzabrutinib (BTK inhibitor — Phase 3 data; reversible inhibitor with better platelet function profile than ibrutinib); iptacopan (complement factor B inhibitor — Phase 3 APLASTIC ANEMIA; ITP Phase 2); anti-CD38 (mezagitamab) for refractory ITP; CAR-T targeting pathogenic autoimmune B cells
remission.notes: Manually curated from landmark trials, registries, and guidelines (disease_db_100.json). Protected from automated overwrite.
